revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The two larger burial mounds were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Okadayama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.matsue.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/bunkasportsbu_bunkazaika/rekishi_bunkazai/3/1/1/2706.html Matsue city Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The two larger burial mounds were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The two larger burial mounds were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Okadayama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.matsue.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/bunkasportsbu_bunkazaika/rekishi_bunkazai/3/1/1/2706.html Matsue city Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

Kofun No. 2 is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[Matsue Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Okadayama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.matsue.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/bunkasportsbu_bunkazaika/rekishi_bunkazai/3/1/1/2706.html Matsue city Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Okadayama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.matsue.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/bunkasportsbu_bunkazaika/rekishi_bunkazai/3/1/1/2706.html Matsue city Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: tag pages missing wikidata links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Okadayama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.matsue.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/bunkasportsbu_bunkazaika/rekishi_bunkazai/3/1/1/2706.html Matsue city Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okadayama Kofun Group]][[en:Okadayama Kofun]][[ja:岡田山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Okadayama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.matsue.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/bunkasportsbu_bunkazaika/rekishi_bunkazai/3/1/1/2706.html Matsue city Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okadayama Kofun Group]][[en:Okadayama Kofun]][[ja:岡田山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Okadayama Kofun Group]][[en:Okadayama Kofun]][[ja:岡田山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Okadayama Kofun Group]][[en:Okadayama Kofun]][[ja:岡田山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Okadayama Kofun Group]][[en:Okadayama Kofun]][[ja:岡田山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Matsue, Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okadayama Kofun | native_name =岡田山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 岡田山古墳1号墳石室開口部.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okadayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|133|05|29|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
