The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar [[:en:Corbel|Corbel]] construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel}} construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Okadayama Kofun'''|岡田山古墳|}} is a cluster of seven large and small [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Okusa-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel}} construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel}} construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel|1=Corbel}} construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>

The two larger Kofuns were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1965.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163561|title=岡田山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> The artifacts from this site have been collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1985.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/207000|title=出雲岡田山古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== In the Okadayama Kofun cluster, Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 2 are relatively large, but the other five are extremely small. Kofun No. 1 is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) tumulus, with a total length of 24 meters, with the posterior portion about 14 meters wide and about 4 meters high, and the anterior portion about 14 meters wide and 3.5 meters high. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber with a total length of 5.6 meters, containing a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. It is believed to have been built in the middle of the 6th century. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1915. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included decorative iron swords, ironware, silver rings, gilt bronze round beads, horse harnesses, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. The inscription 'Nukatabe no Omi' was discovered using X-ray photography on the blade of one of the swords in 1983. One of the bronze mirrors also was found to have an inscription, which dated it to the [[Han dynasty#Eastern Han|Later Han dynasty]]. The burial chamber shows a strong connection to [[Kyushu]], where a similar {{ill|Corbel|en|Corbel|WD=Q12537|lt=Corbel|1=Corbel}} construction technique has been used.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No. 2 is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) style tumulus located 30 meters south of Kofun No. 1. It has a diameter of approximately 43 meters and a height of approximately 6.5 meters and is the third largest in Shimane Prefecture. No excavations have been conducted.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the site is preserved as the Yakumotatsu Fudoki-no-oka [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], which is approximately 5.5 kilometers, or 13 minutes by car, from {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
<gallery> 岡田山古墳1号墳.JPG|Kofun No.1 岡田山古墳1号墳石室.JPG|Kofun No.1Burial Chamber 岡田山古墳2号墳.JPG|Kofun No.2 </gallery>
